rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDR RABINOVICH VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY DBA FIRST
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 7, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, TRUSTEE FOR, WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must specifically identify the particular defendants against whom each cause of action is directed. (See California Rule of Court 2.112.) The complaint, as a whole, is unclear as to what harm plaintiff has suffered and what legal remedies plaintiff is entitled to: Plaintiff must allege facts that address each element of each cause of action. Misrepresentation and fraud must be alleged with particularity. Plaintiff must allege that he performed on the contract to state a claim for breach of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff fails to allege the elements of a conspiracy. To support a claim for unfair business practices, plaintiff must specify unfair, fraudulent or unlawful acts that legally apply to the defendants. Plaintiff granted one final leave to amend to state a cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FREMONT BANK A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION VS. BOEGER THOMAS MUTLIMEDIA A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 7, 2009, line 11, PLAINTIFF FREMONT BANK MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Summary Judgment granted as to defendant Harmon and Defendant trustees, however, court requires information relating to those defendants who have filed for bankruptcy protection and any stays. Requests for default judgment denied. Plaintiff may seek attorney fees in a properly noticed motion. Hearing required to determine bankruptcy issues. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LILLIAN GRADILLAS et al VS. NITELIFE EXPRESS INC., DBA VIP CLUB BUS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 7, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT NITELIFE EXPRESS INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff to clearly allege where defendant's duty derives from for negligence, allege a statutory violation for the fourth cause of action, and allege the type of contact and performance for fifth cause of action. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action for attorney's fees which is not a cause of action but a form of relief. =(302/CWW)
